theme: Iterative matrix balancing (Sinkhorn) accelerated on nearly block-decomposable instances within an iteration budget.
title: Coupling-Matrix Balancer: Block-Aware Over-Relaxed Sinkhorn Under an Operation Budget
strategies: minimal effort: one blind row sweep then one blind column sweep, ignore the rest of the budget
obvious textbook recipe: plain alternating row/column Sinkhorn sweeps (omega=1) for the whole budget
adaptive over-relaxation only: tune omega on plain row/col sweeps without ever looking for block structure

block-aware: detect near-reducible blocks from the coupling pattern, settle within-block ratios with a few plain sweeps, then spend the rest of the budget on over-relaxed coarse block corrections -- falling back to plain sweeps when the regime-detector finds no real block structure
